key_insight: FEVessel learns a mesh-independent 3D pressure-vessel solver by pretraining on total potential energy, avoiding FEM labels while generalizing across materials, geometries, loads, and poor meshes.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical surrogate for industrial pressure-vessel analysis that can speed up simulation, reduce mesh-quality sensitivity, and reduce manual mesh repair.
method: Represents each vessel as a point cloud with coordinate, material, and load features.
Pretrains a Transolver operator on total potential energy rather than supervised FEM displacement labels.
Uses the learned prediction to warm-start iterative FEM solvers for faster convergence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 1.35% relative displacement error, 2.07% strain error, a 9.2× wall-clock speedup, and <3.66% error even on inverted/sliver meshes.
watch_item: The approach still depends on a pretrained operator and may require careful transfer tuning when moving to substantially different vessel families or physics settings.
paper_title: FEVessel: Mesh-Independent Analysis of 3D Pressure Vessels with the Label-Free Pretrained Finite Element Method
